question_title: Will the interest in “uf” change between 2026-04-01 and 2026-04-09 according to Google Trends?
summary: The search term "uf" (University of Florida) is primarily driven by UF sports interest. Florida was the #1 seed in the 2026 NCAA tournament but was upset in the second round, eliminating the biggest search driver. The Final Four (April 4-6) does not include UF. The April 1 value is 55, with the ±3 threshold requiring April 9 to be in [52,58] for "Doesn't change." The non-event weekday baseline for "uf" in this date range appears to be ~52-55, meaning the value is near the lower boundary of the "Doesn't change" band. UF baseball has regular games in the window (Apr 2-4, 7) providing minor search interest. The Orange & Blue Game (football spring game) is April 11, just outside the window, which could generate some anticipation. The mixture model simulation and contextual analysis favor "Doesn't change" as the most likely outcome, with "Decreases" as the next most probable if the value reverts to baseline (~50-51), and "Increases" least likely but possible with a minor event catalyst.

factors.description: UF eliminated from NCAA tournament in Round 2 - biggest search driver removed. No UF basketball games in April 1-9 window.
Non-event weekday baseline for 'uf' in resolution range is ~50-55. Apr 1 at 55 is at upper edge; mean-reversion toward 52-53 expected.
±3 threshold covers 7-point band [52-58] centered near the non-event baseline. This is a relatively wide band given calm-period volatility.
Orange & Blue Game (football spring game) is April 11, just 2 days after our window. Pre-event anticipation searches could push Apr 8-9 values up.
UF baseball has regular games Apr 2-4 and Apr 7. These generate minor search interest but not major spikes.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty of ±2 points on both dates. Could shift resolution category either way.
2025 same period: Apr 1=43 → Apr 9=46 (change +3, 'Doesn't change') BUT that included UF winning the national championship. Without such a catalyst in 2026, less upward pressure.

Empirical 8-day change base rates from resolution range show 40% increases, 0% doesn't change, 60% decreases - but heavily influenced by post-tournament spike decay, not representative of calm period.

reasoning:
**Forecast: "uf" Google Trends interest, April 1-9, 2026**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| Increases | 25% |
| Doesn't change | **42%** |
| Decreases | 33% |

**Key reasoning:**

- **Apr 1 value: 55** → "Doesn't change" requires Apr 9 in [52, 58]; "Decreases" needs <52; "Increases" needs >58
- **UF eliminated from NCAA tournament** in Round 2 upset. The Final Four (Apr 4-6) features Michigan, Arizona, UConn, Illinois — not Florida. The biggest search driver is gone.
- **Non-event baseline ~52-55** in this scale. The Apr 1 value of 55 sits at the upper edge, suggesting slight mean-reversion toward 52-53, which is right at the "Decreases" boundary.
- **Orange & Blue Game (Apr 11)** is just outside the window but could generate some anticipation searches on Apr 8-9
- **UF baseball** has regular games (Apr 2-4, 7) providing steady but modest search interest
- **SerpAPI measurement uncertainty** (±2 points) adds fuzziness near the threshold boundaries

"Doesn't change" is most likely because the ±3 band [52-58] covers most of the expected non-event baseline range. "Decreases" is a strong second because mean-reversion to ~50-51 would exceed the threshold. "Increases" is least likely but non-trivial due to Orange & Blue anticipation and possible unexpected news.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “uf” change between 2026-04-01 and 2026-04-09 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43007)
[2026 NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_NCAA_Division_I_men's_basketball_tournament)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=uf&date=2026-03-10%202026-04-09](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=uf&date=2026-03-10%202026-04-09) for 2026-04-09 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-01. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "uf",
  "date": "2026-03-10 2026-04-09",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
